content:
Hey! I'm looking for the ability to create busses from Rust using something like bus_register (see here). I searched around the mailing-list and here in Zulip, and I didn't see anything that looked related. I wanted to check and see if there was any ongoing work I didn't find before getting started working on it myself.

My needs from this abstraction are pretty slim compared to what's offered on the C side, so, were I to end up working on it, it would probably start out pretty bare-bones. That said, if this work would be useful to others, please do let me know what you would want and I will do my best to include it should I get to that point.

No, right now there is not any bindings to write a whole bus subsystem in rust, sorry. Feel free to submit the bindings if you need this, along with the new bus subsystem that uses it, and we will be glad to review it.

What bus are you writing that isn't currently already supported by an existing in-kernel bus subsystem?

No, right now there is not any bindings to write a whole bus subsystem in rust, sorry. Feel free to submit the bindings if you need this, along with the new bus subsystem that uses it, and we will be glad to review it.

Sounds good. Thank you!

What bus are you writing that isn't currently already supported by an existing in-kernel bus subsystem?

I'm interested in writing a proof-of-concept port of the HyperV vmbus to Rust in order to (better) facilitate writing HV drivers in rust (for what it's worth, I'm employed at Microsoft). It's my understanding that there's no appetite to have duplicate C and Rust code upstream simultaneously, so this work is pretty much experimental. That's part of why I wanted to see if these abstractions would be useful to others: so that I can make sure to submit them if so.

I would love to see ALL of the hyperv drivers move over to rust (and told the MS developers in person this years ago). We have no problem dropping existing .c drivers for .rs drivers if the maintainers of the subsystem agree to, so work with them to make this happen.

That being said, it's probably easier to do a rust binding for the hyperv bus first, redo the drivers, and then consider the bus conversion itself, that's the most minor part of all of that work.

Good luck!

Microsoft even gave us a support statement years ago about it (thanks!):

“Microsoft's Linux Systems Group is interested in contributing to getting Rust into Linux kernel. Hopefully we will be able to submit select Hyper-V drivers written in Rust in the coming months.”

https://rust-for-linux.com/industry-and-academia-support#Microsoft
